recording_notes: Monitor recording; Penrose drain on foam block with two target marks. Oblique angle limits mm calibration on placement and exact wrap count on first throw. Scored task time runs from first instrument visibility (~frame 133) to completion of both intracorporeal cuts (~frame ~2990+).

frame_analyses.description: First instrument becomes visible and the needle is brought toward the Penrose for the initial setup.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task timing begins here; entry is controlled but there is a brief setup delay before the first bite.

frame_analyses.description: The suture has been passed through the marked points and is being drawn through to equalize the tails.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Bite placement appears close to both target dots, with gentle handling and no avulsion.

frame_analyses.description: The first knot throw is being constructed with a loop forming around the opposing instrument.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop geometry is most consistent with a double-wrap first throw, although the camera angle partially obscures the exact wrap count.

frame_analyses.description: The first throw is being seated down onto the Penrose and the closure is beginning to approximate.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The knot is advanced in a controlled fashion, but it takes several regrasping motions to fully set.

frame_analyses.description: A second throw is being laid with the instruments reoriented and the loop passed in the opposite direction.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching appears to occur here; the throw looks like a single half-hitch and lies flatter than the first.

frame_analyses.description: The third required throw is being formed with another loop passed around the opposing instrument.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alternation appears preserved, though the exact lead hand is only moderately clear because the performance is filmed off a monitor at an angle.

frame_analyses.description: The final knot is being squared and there appears to be an additional reinforcing pass or retightening maneuver before cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: This likely improves security but costs time and suggests the third-throw sequence was not completed as efficiently as it could have been.

frame_analyses.description: The cutter severs the intracorporeal tails and short residual ends remain adjacent to the knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both ends appear cut inside the trainer; no immediate loosening is visible after the cut.

knot_assessments.notes: Best estimate is a left-leading double throw; wrap count partially obscured but sequence most consistent with a surgeon's knot.
Second throw appears to reverse direction and lay as a single half-hitch with improved squaring.
Third throw appears left-leading and is followed by probable extra reinforcing manipulation before cutting; final knot still appears stable.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Both bites appear close to target dots; oblique monitor recording limits mm calibration.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gap after final tightening; drain remains attached; final knot appears stable after cutting.

technique_summary: Clean, non-zeroing completion. Placement accurate, drain intact, final knot stable, both ends cut intracorporeally. Main score loss is efficiency in the late task — prolonged third-throw/final-setting sequence with probable reinforcing pass, plus delayed cutter introduction after the knot was already acceptable.
improvement_suggestions: Shorten setup between later throws by organizing post and working end before wrapping.
Square later half-hitches more decisively to avoid the extra reinforcing pass seen before cutting.
Introduce the cutter sooner after the final accepted knot; delay before tail division is the single biggest non-knot time cost.
strengths: Initial bites close to both target dots with only small estimated deviation.
Drain stayed attached with no avulsion or gross tissue trauma.
Final knot secure; tails successfully cut inside the trainer.
